requirement_verbatim: Install two mandatory gates before another feature handoff. The merge gate should require an agent-owned PR, protected main, clean macOS tests, SwiftLint, Periphery, CodeQL, and an independent read-only GPT-5.6 Sol review. The handoff gate should launch the signed app on Adam's Mac, execute the exact visible requirement, capture screenshots/video and test results bound to the commit, and prevent the agent from ending its task without that evidence. Keep the public repository off Adam's Mac as a GitHub self-hosted runner.
visible_surface: The Harness GitHub pull request checks and protection state, followed by the signed Harness macOS app on Adam's Mac for every product change.
expected_visible_result: No product pull request can merge without the hosted checks, independent Sol verdict, and signed Mac handoff status; no agent can end a product-change task without a valid commit-bound evidence manifest; the final main commit visibly attests that its tree exactly matches the verified pull-request head.
critical_flow: Open an agent-owned pull request whose exact commit contains Adam's literal acceptance contract.
Run hosted macOS tests, SwiftLint, changed-line Periphery, and Swift/Python CodeQL; revalidate raw test evidence in fresh protected-base jobs.
Run GPT-5.6 Sol through Adam's ChatGPT/Codex subscription against an inert read-only base/head bundle.
Use Adam's trusted local identity to verify exact hosted workflow paths, events, runs, creators, and CodeQL jobs before aggregating hosted evidence.
Build and verify the signed app, execute the exact named XCUITest while recording video, and require a direct accepted-only Fuseki preflight plus a separate production-route Ollama synthesis proof.
Rerun the exact assertion after a normal launch, bind the exact candidate PID and window, validate decoded nonblank media, and hash all evidence into the manifest.
Require protected main and the installed harness-merge command to reject missing, spoofed, stale, or failed evidence; permit merge commits only and attest the final tree.
Require the Stop hook to reject task completion when any repository change lacks current handoff evidence.
required_proof: Adversarial gate tests with complete inventory, fresh-job macOS evidence validation, deterministic Swift tests, SwiftLint, changed-line Periphery, and CodeQL.
A structured independent GPT-5.6 Sol review bound to the exact commit.
Signed app identity, exact UI xcresults, initial and final screenshots, decoded nonblank video, TCC preflight, exact PID/window Accessibility proof, and live Fuseki/Ollama evidence.
Read-back of protected-main, Actions, merge-mode, and control-bundle state; trusted-workflow identity proofs; merge rejection probes; exact release-tree attestation; and zero self-hosted runners.
risk_and_authority_boundaries: This committed JSON is the sole authoritative acceptance contract; pull-request prose is a display copy and edits cannot change release authority. Status names alone are never authority: Adam's trusted local aggregator verifies protected workflow identity and exact evidence before his local identity issues merge authority, and only the installed harness-merge command may merge. The Sol reviewer is read-only and is not the release oracle. Adam blocks model API keys and uses his ChatGPT/Codex subscription. Adam's authenticated macOS session and dblaira GitHub administrator identity are trusted operator boundaries; the gates prevent accidental, careless, stale, unreviewed, and proposal-controlled handoffs but do not claim resistance to a malicious actor already controlling that identity. Signing, screen capture, app state, provider state, and accepted-graph authority remain distinct. Adam's Mac is never a self-hosted runner for this public repository.
threat_model: Adam's authenticated macOS session and dblaira GitHub administrator identity are trusted. The gates prevent accidental, careless, stale, and unreviewed handoffs; they do not claim cryptographic resistance to a malicious actor already controlling Adam's administrator credential, and they do not require Touch ID.
